utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 corresponding keys. Each item specifies what it is and includes a cost. The format requires: for each of the first four, a description of the type (health/property/travel/additional) and costs—these are present. The total_budget requires a complete monthly/annual cost breakdown; the plan provides a total monthly cost but no annual figure. However, the item is still present with a monthly cost. Given the instruction to be fully specified with all details, the absence of an annual breakdown may be considered incomplete. The other four items appear fully specified as per the format (description + costs). Therefore, 4 are fully specified, and 1 (total_budget) is partially specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three items by name: EliteCare Premium Plan (health), LifeFirst Standard Coverage (property), and PrimeTravel Premium Plan (travel). I searched for these exact names in the ground-truth ratings. EliteCare Premium Plan appears under primary_health_coverage (physical_health) with rating 6. LifeFirst Standard Coverage appears under property_protection (home_content) with rating 9. PrimeTravel Premium Plan appears under mobility_coverage (travel_insurance) with rating 6. No additional services were selected, so only these three need ratings.
